item.title: Dead Code Elimination: A Linker's Perspective
item.description:
Dead Code Elimination: A Linker’s Perspective #

Linkers are crucial components of the build process, responsible for combining object files and libraries into a final executable. A key, often unseen, function they perform is dead code elimination. This process removes code that is never called, reducing the executable size and potentially improving performance. Let’s demonstrate this with a practical example.

Link Dependent *.o files in *.a #

1. Setting the Stage: add.c and minus.c

item.title: 共享库，what? why? how?
item.description:
Introduction of Shared Libraries #

Introduction #

Recently, while improving the content related to linkers and loaders in my ebook “Design and Implementation of a Go Language Debugger,” I also read the book “Linkers and Loaders” and gained a deeper understanding of how linkers and loaders work. I’d like to take this opportunity to share and review some interesting points within it.

The Big Picture #

To improve code reuse, modular design, reduce redundant disk space usage, and optimize memory usage, the concept of shared libraries emerged. They can be strictly divided into two types: Static Shared Library and Dynamic Shared Library. The former uses static linking and static loading, while the latter uses dynamic linking, and can use both static and dynamic loading methods.

item.title: cgo共享库的编译与加载
item.description:
go语言提供了c、go互相调用的能力。

比较常见的是在go中借助c头文件、共享库来调用c代码，通过cgo指令可以指定linkers要连接的共享库的搜索路径。下面是一个示例。

file: add.h

#ifndef _ADD_H_
#define _ADD_H_
int add(int, int);
#endif

file: add.c

int add(int n, int m)
{
return n + m;
}

首先将add.c编译成共享库：

gcc -c -fpic add.c
gcc -shared -o libadd.so add.o

file: main.go

import "fmt"
/*
# cgo CFLAGS: -I${SRCDIR}
# cgo LDFLAGS: -L${SRCDIR} -ladd
#include "add.h"
*/
import "C"
func main() {
fmt.Printf("1 + 2 = %v\n", C.add(1, 2))
}

import "C"必须紧跟在cgo相关的指令、说明后面，它们以注释的形式包括起来，cgo编译器会处理这里的信息。如cgo指令 -I${SRCDIR}会指导从哪里寻找add.h，cgo -L${SRCDIR}会指导从哪里寻找libadd.so。
